screenshots.note: Live screenshots captured via Puppeteer/Chromium from https://osortega.github.io/bloberto-office/

carry_over_status.error_workers_invisible: STILL UNFIXED — deskOccupants never includes error-status workers
carry_over_status.shortcut_toast_no_dismiss: STILL UNFIXED — no button in ShortcutToast JSX
carry_over_status.vibe_labels_in_effect: STILL UNFIXED — App.jsx:1102, inline const inside useEffect
carry_over_status.footer_quote_skip: STILL UNFIXED — App.jsx:1471, `? 1 : p + 1` never returns to 0
carry_over_status.sub_readable_text: STILL UNFIXED — Office.css has 0.32rem–0.48rem text on floor elements

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #2] JSON.parse on vibe history is still unguarded
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1114: `const history = raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : []`. If localStorage key 'bloberto-vibe-history' holds corrupted or non-JSON data (e.g., from a different app version, storage migration, or manual tampering), this throws a SyntaxError. That error is NOT caught by safeRead — safeRead only wraps localStorage.getItem, not parsing. The whole vibe-streak useEffect blows up, taking the activity log synthetic event with it. Fix: wrap in try/catch: `let history = []; try { history = raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : [] } catch { history = [] }`. Two-line fix. We've talked about this for two reviews now. Come on.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #2] Error-status workers are still invisible on the Office floor
suggestions.description: Office.jsx deskOccupants (lines 1310-1323) is built from workingWorkers (status==='working') + idleWorkers (status==='idle') + ghostRoster. Workers with status==='error' match NONE of these — they're in activeWorkers from App.jsx's filter (`working || idle || error`), so they reach Office as props, but then fall through the desk-assignment logic completely. The hasError check at line 1422 can NEVER be true because no error worker is ever in deskOccupants. All that beautiful error CSS (desk--error, desk-lamp--error, DESK_SCREEN_SVG.*_error, char--glitch, char__error-badge) is dead code in practice. Fix: add `const errorWorkers = nonMgr.filter(w => w.status === 'error')` and slot them into deskOccupants after idleWorkers.

suggestions.title: Bloberto speech bubble overflows office-floor container on mobile
suggestions.description: The manager Character is positioned at top: 4% inside .office-floor. The speech bubble uses `position: absolute; bottom: calc(100% + 8px)` which places it ABOVE the character — so roughly at top: -1% to 0% of the floor. On mobile (375px wide) where the office floor has reduced height, the bubble escapes the top boundary and renders in the tab-toggle area. The .office-floor has `overflow: clip; overflow-clip-margin: 20px` which allows content up to 20px outside to show, but the bubble can extend further than that. Visible in the mobile screenshot: the Bloberto speech bubble partially overlaps the tab buttons. Fix: add `overflow: hidden` or clamp the bubble's top position within the container.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER] ShortcutToast has no dismiss button for mouse/touch users
suggestions.description: The ShortcutToast (App.jsx lines 631-686) auto-dismisses after 7s or on any shortcut keypress. Touch users who don't use keyboard shortcuts are forced to wait the full 7 seconds. The toast is already hidden on pointer:coarse + max-width:640px, but there's no X button or tap-to-dismiss for desktop mouse users who don't trigger shortcut keys. Keyboard users get immediate dismiss. Fix: add a small close button inside the toast JSX.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER] VIBE_LABELS constant re-declared inside useEffect on every vibe change
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1102: `const VIBE_LABELS = { 'after-hours': 'After Hours', ... }` is declared inside the teamVibe.key useEffect. This object is identical on every run and is only used two lines later. It allocates a new object reference on every vibe transition. Extract it as a module-level constant. It's a trivially small allocation, but it's also a trivially simple fix — and leaving it here is just sloppy.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER] Footer quote cycle permanently drops the vibe-reactive tagline after cycling through all bonus quotes
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1471: `setQuoteBonus(p => p >= BONUS_TAGLINES.length ? 1 : p + 1)`. When the counter reaches BONUS_TAGLINES.length (8), it resets to 1 — skipping 0. But quoteBonus === 0 is the ONLY state that shows the vibe-reactive FOOTER_TAGLINES entry. After one full cycle, the user is locked out of the dynamic tagline forever. Fix: change `? 1 :` to `? 0 :` and update the counter display accordingly.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER] Sub-readable text sizes persist on the office floor
suggestions.description: Office.css still has multiple floor elements at 0.32rem–0.48rem: char--idle .char__name (0.32rem), desk__nameplate (0.38rem/0.44rem at mobile breakpoints), wb-vibe-text (0.55rem at 480px), idle-duration (0.26rem). At 0.26rem on a physical mobile device this is approximately 3–4px — not even decorative, just technically visible. The very-small-text is a deliberate aesthetic choice for the isometric feel, but 0.26rem crosses from 'tiny but cute' into 'actively inaccessible'. Bump the floor to 0.5rem minimum for legible elements.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER] isTouchDevice is a module-load snapshot, not a live media query
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 692: `const isTouchDevice = typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches`. This evaluates ONCE when the module loads. Users who connect/disconnect a mouse (common on tablets, Surface devices, iPads with Magic Keyboard) will get stale results until a full page reload. Fix: either use `window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches` inline at the point of use, or add a matchMedia change listener inside a useEffect.

suggestions.title: Idle bubble index is a 190-character unreadable inline expression
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 813: the idle micro-bubble array index is computed entirely inline inside JSX — 190+ characters including a reduce-based hash of the worker ID, all on one line inside a className-bearing div. This is not unreadable because it's clever; it's unreadable because it's lazy. Extract to a named function like `getIdleBubbleIndex(workerId, bubbleArray)` at the module level. Future maintainers (and linters) will thank you.

suggestions.title: completionToast and plantToast use copy-pasted inline style blocks
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1190-1212 and Office.jsx lines 1329-1351 both implement a fixed-bottom-center toast using virtually identical inline style objects (position:fixed, bottom:2rem/5.5rem, left:50%, transform, background, border, borderRadius, padding, zIndex, whiteSpace:nowrap, animation). This pattern is duplicated across two files. Extract as a shared `.toast-overlay` CSS class — or at minimum a shared inline style constant — to avoid the two implementations drifting apart over time.
